diagrams.title: Multi-tape TM to Single-tape TM Tape Encoding
diagrams.description: Shows how k virtual tapes are encoded on a single physical tape using separator symbols (#) and position markers (X, Y) for head positions.
elements: Separator symbols (#) delimit each virtual tape
Position markers (X under head on tape i)
Content of each virtual tape in separate sections
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how the simulation maintains multiple tape contents and head positions on a single tape, enabling the O(n²) time simulation.

diagrams.title: NDTM Configuration Tree with BFS Exploration
diagrams.description: Tree diagram showing computation paths of an NDTM. Root is initial configuration. Each branch represents a possible non-deterministic move. DTM explores level by level (BFS).
elements: Branching nodes: configurations with multiple possible transitions
BFS ordering: level 0, then level 1, then level 2...
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate how a deterministic TM systematically explores all non-deterministic branches using breadth-first search to simulate an NDTM.

diagrams.title: Universal Turing Machine Input Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the combined input to a UTM: encoding of TM M on the left, encoding of input w on the right, with appropriate delimiters.
elements: Encoding of M: states, transition function δ as table, tape alphabet Γ
Encoding of w: sequence of input symbols
State markers and tape symbol encoding
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how a single machine can take 'programs' (encoded TMs) as data, the foundation of stored-program computers.
